# Rayact

> Rayact is a cross-platform React renderer with a native raylib + QuickJS backend. You write React (with react-navigation, CSS, Material components) and it renders natively on desktop, Android, iOS, and the web — no DOM, no platform WebView.

## Guide

- [Getting started](https://rayact.dev/guide/getting-started): Rayact runs your React app on a prebuilt native engine — you write the app, the engine is already compiled. This guide goes from zero to a running app on desktop, a device, and the web.
- [Installation](https://rayact.dev/guide/install): Rayact splits into small @rayact/ JavaScript packages plus per-platform

## Pages

- [Multi-Window System Documentation](https://rayact.dev/multi-window-system): The Rayact multi-window system allows a single QuickJS runtime instance to manage multiple windows/activities/viewcontrollers, enabling native navigation stacks and complex multi-window applications.

## Reference

- [CLI reference](https://rayact.dev/reference/cli): The rayact command (from @rayact/cli) drives development, building, and running.
- [Configuration: `rayact.config.json`](https://rayact.dev/reference/config): Schema for rayact.config.json. Fields omitted fall back to built-in defaults; only set a field to override a default.
- [Packages & platforms](https://rayact.dev/reference/packages): Rayact is a monorepo of small @rayact/ packages plus per-platform prebuilts.
- [Container format (`.rayactpack`)](https://rayact.dev/reference/rayactpack): Release builds ship a single self-contained container instead of a loose tree of

## Full corpus

- [llms-full.txt](https://rayact.dev/llms-full.txt): every page concatenated as Markdown
